title: Syngonium Green Mottled Plant,Indoor Plant,houseplant in Pot,Easy-Care
features: The Syngonium Green Mottled is a striking tropical houseplant known for its arrow-shaped leaves with beautiful natural mottling. This plant comes already trained on a moss pole, helping it grow upright while developing fuller foliage.

This easy-care plant prefers bright, indirect light and moderate watering, making it perfect for both beginners and experienced plant enthusiasts. Each plant is supplied healthy and well-rooted in a nursery pot, ready to grow and flourish in your home.
Light: Provide bright, indirect sunlight. Avoid direct sun exposure, which can scorch the leaves. Water: Water when the top inch of soil feels dry. Reduce watering during the winter months.
Temperature: Keep your Syngonium in a room with temperatures between 60-75°F (15-24°C).
Fertilizing: Feed with a balanced, water-soluble fertilizer during the growing season (spring and summer). Size:20 cm tall
description: The Syngonium Green Mottled is a striking tropical houseplant known for its arrow-shaped leaves with beautiful natural mottling. This plant comes already trained on a moss pole, helping it grow upright while developing fuller foliage. At around 20cm tall, it’s a great size for tabletops, desks, and plant shelves. This low-maintenance plant is perfect for beginners and experienced plant lovers alike, thriving in bright, indirect light with occasional watering. Each plant is supplied healthy, well-rooted, and potted in a nursery pot. Leaf patterns and shades may vary slightly, making every plant unique. Please note: As this is a living plant, each one is unique, and leaf colouration may vary slightly. Plant Care Tips: Light: Bright, indirect sunlight Water: Keep soil lightly moist but not soggy Temperature: 15–25°C Humidity: Average indoor humidity or higher Please Note: The plant comes in the nursery pot, which may differ from the main picture. Please note that the shape and size may vary due to the season and growth stage. Plants Orders placed after Thursday 12pm are dispatched on Monday to ensure plant health during transit.
